product: Cisco Meraki Dashboard API v1 — organization inventory over REST

auth.note: the v1 standard bearer header on every request (v0's X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key is obsolete); the key must never appear in error text

redirects.behavior: api.meraki.com redirects API calls to the organization's dashboard shard (e.g. nNNN.meraki.com); GETs redirect with 302, non-GETs with 307/308; clients must follow redirects themselves because many HTTP libraries drop the Authorization header or downgrade the verb
redirects.trust_rule: preserve the Authorization header ONLY when the redirect target host is a trusted Meraki host (default suffixes: .meraki.com, .meraki.ca, .meraki.cn, .meraki.in, .gov-meraki.com; contexts may inject additional trusted authorities); follow untrusted redirects WITHOUT the Authorization header
redirects.fixture_note: the loopback fixture injects the trusted authority host:port explicitly since 127.0.0.1 can never match a Meraki suffix

pagination.style: RFC 5988/8288 Link response header, comma-separated relations among first/prev/next/last
pagination.rel_forms: rel appears both quoted (rel="next") and unquoted (rel=next); both must parse
pagination.rule: follow the rel=next URL verbatim, never rebuilding its query string

headers.Retry-After: seconds to wait before the next request

errors: API rate limit exceeded for organization

rate_limit.client_rule: wait exactly Retry-After seconds via the injected SleepAction, retry at most RetryLimit times, then throw naming the 429 status

errors.client_rule: throw with the status and the joined messages; never the API key

success.body: array of {serial, name, model, networkId, productType, mac, firmware, ...}

stable_output.shape: {organizationId, networks: [{id, name, productTypes}], devices: [{serial, name, model, networkId, productType}]}
stable_output.ordering: networks sorted by id ordinal ascending, devices by serial ordinal ascending, productTypes sorted ordinal; keys emitted exactly in the pinned order; compact (no whitespace) JSON
stable_output.reason: output feeds a diff-based CMDB import; byte-stability is part of the contract

mock_fixture.pagination_marker: cursorcheck query parameter proves Link URLs are followed verbatim
